title: rubus stenothyrsos
intro: Rubus stenothyrsos, also known as the New Zealand thimbleberry, is a species of bramble that belongs to the rose family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus stenothyrsos include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Rubus stenothyrsos can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
